transformation_type: palladium-mediated dehydrogenation of silyl enol ethers to enones and enals
mechanism_type: Pd(II)-mediated oxidation of silyl enol ethers via β-hydride elimination and reoxidation of palladium
reactant_pattern_text: acyclic or cyclic silyl enol ether, or allyl enol carbonate precursor
common_conditions: stoichiometric or catalytic palladium(II) oxidation, often with p-benzoquinone or oxygen co-oxidation
description_short: Named oxidation that converts silyl enol ethers into conjugated enones or enals under Pd(II) conditions.

transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted allylation of carbonyl compounds with allylsilanes
mechanism_type: addition of allylsilane to a Lewis-acid-activated carbonyl through a β-silyl stabilized cationic transition state
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde, ketone, acetal, or oxocarbenium precursor + allylsilane/crotylsilane
product_pattern_text: homoallylic alcohol or related allylated product
common_conditions: low temperature under Lewis acid activation; allylsilane substitution pattern controls syn/anti outcome
description_short: Carbon–carbon bond-forming allylation of carbonyl compounds using allylsilanes under Lewis-acid activation.

transformation_type: substitution of aryldiazonium salts by halides or pseudohalides
mechanism_type: diazotization followed by copper(I)-mediated dediazoniation and radical-type aryl substitution
reactant_pattern_text: arylamine -> aryldiazonium salt + Cu(I)X or CuCN
product_pattern_text: aryl chloride, bromide, nitrile, iodide, fluoride, or phenol
common_conditions: diazotization with NaNO2/mineral acid or alkyl nitrites followed by copper(I) salt mediated substitution
description_short: Classic arylamine-to-aryl halide or nitrile conversion through diazonium-salt intermediates.

transformation_type: hydrazoic-acid-mediated rearrangement of carbonyl compounds and carboxylic acids
mechanism_type: acid-promoted azide addition and rearrangement through azidoalcohol or acyl azide/isocyanate intermediates
reactant_pattern_text: ketone, aldehyde, or carboxylic acid + HN3 under strong acid
product_pattern_text: amide, lactam, nitrile, or amine after rearrangement/hydrolysis
common_conditions: strong Brønsted or Lewis acid catalysis; migration-controlled rearrangement and frequent ring expansion of cyclic ketones
description_short: Azide-based rearrangement that converts carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids into nitrogen-containing products.

transformation_type: base-mediated acylation of amines and alcohols with acid chlorides or anhydrides
mechanism_type: interfacial nucleophilic acyl substitution under aqueous inorganic or organic basic conditions
reactant_pattern_text: amine or alcohol + acid chloride/anhydride under basic biphasic conditions
common_conditions: two-phase aqueous base or organic base conditions; selective amidation or esterification by nucleophilic acyl substitution
description_short: Foundational acylation protocol for converting amines and alcohols into amides and esters under basic conditions.
